findings.hypothesis_text: In MOSAIC OV, which spatial regions show the highest CD8+ T cell density?
findings.effect: CD8+ T cells concentrate at ovarian tumor borders and in CXCL13-rich regions adjacent to tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) and peritumoral stroma, consistent with Kasikova et al. (Nat Commun 2024) and follow-up TLS studies in HGSOC.

resources.citation: Kasikova, L., Rakova, J., Hensler, M. et al. Tertiary lymphoid structures and B cells determine clinically relevant T cell phenotypes in ovarian cancer. Nat Commun 15, 2528 (2024).

findings.author: Mahdi
findings.markdown:
## CD8+ T cell density in MOSAIC OV — spatial localization

In HGSOC (MOSAIC OV), CD8+ T cells concentrate at tumor borders and in CXCL13-rich regions adjacent to tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS). This is consistent with Kasikova et al. (Nat Commun 2024), who found CD8+ T cells localized near TLSs and peritumoral regions in HGSOC, and with follow-up TLS studies.

**Validation note:** If K-Pro shows CD8+ T cells clustered at OV tumor borders or in CXCL13-rich regions, this is consistent with Kasikova et al. and follow-up TLS studies, which found CD8+ T cells concentrated near TLSs and peritumoral regions in HGSOC.

**Citation:** Kasikova, L., Rakova, J., Hensler, M. et al. Tertiary lymphoid structures and B cells determine clinically relevant T cell phenotypes in ovarian cancer. *Nat Commun* 15, 2528 (2024). https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-46873-w

findings.hypothesis_text: Do MOSAIC DLBCL tumors show organized follicle-like structures (CXCL13+ B cell aggregates) spatially, and which patients have them?
findings.effect: MOSAIC DLBCL tumors show patient-specific organized CXCL13+ B-cell aggregates resembling tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS), with prevalence and intensity varying markedly across patients; consistent with Qin et al. (Oncol Lett 2021) on TLS and B-cell-related pathways as immunotherapy targets.

resources.citation: Qin M, Jin Y, Pan LY. Tertiary lymphoid structure and B-cell-related pathways: A potential target in tumor immunotherapy. Oncol Lett. 2021 Dec;22(6):836.

findings.author: Aufstin
findings.markdown:
## CXCL13+ follicle-like structures in MOSAIC DLBCL - patient-specific TLS

In MOSAIC DLBCL tumors, organized CXCL13+ B-cell aggregates form follicle-like spatial structures resembling tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS), but prevalence and intensity vary markedly across patients. This spatial organization is patient-specific rather than a universal DLBCL feature.

This is consistent with Qin et al. (Oncol Lett 2021), which reviews TLS and B-cell-related pathways as a potential target in tumor immunotherapy, supporting the clinical and immunotherapeutic relevance of CXCL13+ B-cell aggregates in lymphoma.

**Citation:** Qin M, Jin Y, Pan LY. Tertiary lymphoid structure and B-cell-related pathways: A potential target in tumor immunotherapy. *Oncol Lett* 22(6), 836 (2021). https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8548801/
